customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety signals. Glenn is cooperative, warm, and motivated by a specific bill grievance. His primary driver is the unexplained bill spike ($600–$1,100/month, up from a $300 baseline) which he attributes to a nearby solar field coming online. He is not skeptical of solar — he cleared 69 trees in apparent preparation.
unresolved_concerns: Title/co-owners status unknown — no one confirmed whether Glenn has a spouse or co-owner on the title. Closer should establish this before the proposal.
Prior solar designs unknown — no competitive context captured. Glenn may have existing quotes.
Roof type and age not captured — no DQ check completed.

Glenn's scheduling context: he plans to drop his daughter at school and return home before heading to the office. The 9AM slot is a detour from his normal Monday routine, not a committed block. No computer/no-driving anchor was set.
sensitive_topics_raised: Disabled veteran — 31 surgeries over 20+ years, jaw replaced two weeks ago, returned to work against medical advice.

Glenn's bill spike theory: he believes the nearby solar field caused his bill to double-to-triple. The closer should not dismiss or over-engage this theory — acknowledge it and redirect to what solar does for Glenn's bill regardless of cause.
Glenn mentioned a 30-acre property in Silver Lake, Michigan (heavily wooded, not the subject property) — this came up during the photo-send attempt and is not relevant to the Ohio install.
exact_promises_rep_made: Design team will build options based on the bill photo received.
Energy specialist will bring the design to Glenn on Monday at 9AM.
Confirmation text will be sent after the call.
Meg's personal phone number provided as a direct contact channel.
closer_should_know: Glenn's stated bill range is $600–$1,100/month (rep summarized as ~$800 average — that is the rep's inference, not Glenn's framing). He has a 40x60 detached garage with an open south-facing roof that may be a second installation surface — worth asking whether he wants it included in the design. Title/co-owners status was never confirmed; the closer should establish this at the top of the call before proceeding to the proposal.
